content:
2 min read
Just now

--

Google has overtaken OpenAI as agencies’ preferred AI partner, according to recent research from our friends at Forrester. Predictably, much of the discussion has centred on familiar questions around whether Gemini now has a superior model to ChatGPT. I actually think that’s the wrong question … and the more interesting shift is that marketers are increasingly choosing systems, not models.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

That’s why Google’s momentum feels significant here. Google’s advantage lies in how AI fits into a broader marketing system — one that already includes analytics, advertising, search, cloud infrastructure and productivity tools. It’s a reminder that AI is rapidly becoming infrastructure rather than a standalone capability.
I suspect we’ll see the same pattern elsewhere. The organisations that create the greatest value from AI won’t necessarily be those with access to the most advanced model. They’ll be the ones that redesign how marketing works.
For strategists, that’s an important distinction. Beyond individual tasks — writing copy faster, creating more assets, summarising research or generating campaign ideas — the bigger opportunity is to rethink the marketing operating system itself.
How does consumer insight flow into planning? How do media, creative and CRM learn from one another? How are customer signals captured and fed back into future decisions? Where should AI automate, and where should human judgement remain essential?
The first phase of AI adoption was about tools. The next will be about systems. The organisations that create the most value won’t necessarily be those with access to the most powerful model. They’ll be the ones that redesign how insight, planning, creativity, media, commerce and measurement work together.
That’s what I mean by Marketing as a System — thinking less about individual campaigns or capabilities, and more about designing a marketing function that continuously learns, adapts and makes better decisions. AI is simply accelerating that shift.
